title: Creamy Earthy Argentinian Cheese Empanadas
description: A low‑fat, post‑workout Argentinian empanada packed with creamy Colby Jack, quinoa, and roasted sweet potato for an earthy flavor.
instructions: In a bowl whisk water, olive oil, and salt until combined, then stir in flour to form a smooth dough. Knead briefly, cover, and let rest 10 min.
While dough rests, combine low‑fat milk, shredded Colby Jack, cooked quinoa, roasted sweet potato, egg white, and cilantro in a mixing bowl. Stir until well mixed.
Roll dough into 1.5 cm thick circles on a lightly floured surface. Use a 7 cm round cutter to cut out pinwheels.
Place a heaping tablespoon of filling in the center of each dough circle, fold over to create a half‑moon shape, and press edges to seal. Crimp with a fork for a classic look.
Arrange sealed empanadas on a parchment‑lined baking sheet. Bake for 18‑20 min, or until golden brown.
Remove from oven and let cool for 5 min before serving. Enjoy warm or at room temperature.
